title: Pilogyne perpusilla
taxonomy: Pilogyne perpusilla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It belongs to the genus Pilogyne, which is a group of plants native to tropical Africa. The plant is further classified as a member of the order Sapindales, which includes other trees and shrubs with similar characteristics. This classification places Pilogyne perpusilla within the larger group of rosids, a clade of flowering plants that are thought to have evolved from a common ancestor. Further research is needed to confirm the exact relationships between Pilogyne perpusilla and other related species.

The plant is said to have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which make it effective in treating infections and wounds. In traditional medicine, Pilogyne perpusilla is used to treat fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues such as cough and bronchitis.
